entries.d: A structure built over a river, road, or railway so people can cross
entries.s: We walked across the old stone _____ over the river to get to the other side of the valley.

entries.d: A large, long flow of water that goes to the sea
entries.s: The _____ was very wide and flowing fast, so we definitely needed a boat with an engine to cross it safely.

entries.d: A place where you can look at paintings and sculptures
entries.s: We visited an _____ in the city centre and saw famous paintings by Monet and Picasso on the walls.

entries.d: An open area in a town, usually with buildings around it
entries.s: There is a big beautiful stone fountain in the middle of the old town _____ surrounded by cafés and historic buildings.

entries.d: A large area with grass and trees where people can walk and play
entries.s: Let's meet at the _____ near the slides and the football pitch and play football together after school today.

entries.d: A tall, narrow building or part of a building
entries.s: The tall old church _____ with a bell at the top is the tallest building in our small town — you can see it from 10km away.

entries.d: An area of a city or town
entries.s: The busy shopping _____ in the city centre has lots of nice cafés, boutique shops, and restaurants on every street.

entries.d: A structure with walls and a roof, like a house or school
entries.s: The brand new school _____ has three floors, a big gym, and a modern library for all the students.

entries.d: A road in a city or town with houses and shops
entries.s: Our quiet residential _____ is very peaceful because there are no shops and not much traffic or noise.

entries.d: A place where you buy things
entries.s: There's a cute little _____ near my house on the corner that sells colourful sweets, chocolate, and crisps to children.

entries.d: A large building with many shops inside
entries.s: We went to the huge indoor _____ with 100 shops on three floors to buy new running shoes for school.

entries.d: A large building where people watch sports
entries.s: The giant football _____ was full of 50,000 excited fans cheering loudly for their favourite team.

entries.d: To be destroyed by fire
entries.s: The old abandoned factory _____ completely to the ground last winter during a huge electrical fire.

entries.d: A group of things that have been gathered together
entries.s: My dad has a huge _____ of more than 500 old stamps from many different countries around the world, stored in albums.

entries.d: The group of people who run a country
entries.s: The _____ of our country decided last week to build more free public schools in the remote countryside regions.

entries.d: The building in London where British laws are made
entries.s: We took a photo in front of _____ with the famous Big Ben clock tower when we visited London last summer.

entries.d: Before something happens, ahead of time
entries.s: You should definitely buy your concert tickets _____ — at least two weeks before — because they sell out very quickly.

entries.d: To take a photo of someone or something
entries.s: My photographer sister loves to _____ wild birds and deer in the forest with her big professional camera with a zoom lens.

entries.d: A story performed by actors on a stage
entries.s: Our drama class is going to perform a Shakespeare _____ on the school stage at the end of the school year in June.

entries.d: A building where people are kept as punishment for a crime
entries.s: The convicted thief was sent to _____ for two years by the judge after stealing jewellery from the shop.

entries.d: To move something up to a higher position
entries.s: Please _____ your hand high up in the air if you know the correct answer to the teacher's question.

entries.d: A large black bird
entries.s: A big black _____ was sitting on the wooden fence and cawing loudly at us as we walked past.

entries.d: To go on a stroll, usually for pleasure
entries.s: Let's _____ together in the big park — the warm sunny weather is absolutely lovely today.

entries.d: A building where you go to watch plays and shows
entries.s: We're going to the _____ tonight in Vienna to see a famous musical with live singers and an orchestra playing.

entries.d: What you can see from a place
entries.s: The _____ from our tall hotel room on the 20th floor was truly amazing — we could see the whole blue sea and beach.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with VIEW?

entries.d: A person who goes to see a place or another person
entries.s: The popular history museum has thousands of _____ every single year from countries all over the world.

entries.d: Very exciting and a little bit scary
entries.s: The huge loop-the-loop roller coaster ride at the theme park was really _____ — my heart was beating super fast!

entries.d: Close to an exact number but not exact
entries.s: There are _____ 200 students at our school, give or take about 10 students either way — we don't count exactly.

entries.d: To push air out of your mouth and throat with a loud noise
entries.s: She has been _____ loudly all day since morning because she has a bad chest cold with lots of mucus.

entries.d: Making other people or animals suffer on purpose
entries.s: It is very _____ and wrong to keep wild animals like elephants or lions in small cramped cages at the circus.

entries.d: With nothing or no one inside
entries.s: The kitchen fridge is completely _____ inside — there's no food or drinks left at all. We need to go shopping right away.

entries.d: When your body temperature is higher than normal because you are ill
entries.s: She stayed in bed all day because she had a high _____ of 39 degrees Celsius — the thermometer was way above normal.

entries.d: To tell someone about something that has happened
entries.s: If you see something strange or suspicious in the school building, please _____ it immediately to the nearest teacher.

entries.d: A narrow way for walking
entries.s: Follow this narrow winding _____ carefully through the green forest and you'll arrive at the beautiful hidden lake.

entries.d: Very impressive and beautiful to look at
entries.s: The huge colourful fireworks show on New Year's Eve over the river was absolutely _____ — I've never seen anything so impressive.

entries.d: A place that many tourists visit
entries.s: The big old medieval castle on the hill is the most popular _____ in our town — thousands of tourists visit each summer.

entries.d: To see, feel or do something, especially for the first time
entries.s: On this school trip to a farm, you will _____ real farm life for three days — feeding animals and milking cows.

entries.d: Cars, buses and other vehicles moving on a road
entries.s: There is always a lot of _____ with many cars, buses, and lorries in the city centre during rush hour at 8 am.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with TRAFFIC?

entries.d: Having people from many different countries and cultures
entries.s: Vienna is a very _____ city with many people from all over the world living together — from Turkey, Japan, Nigeria, and more.

entries.d: A written agreement between two people or groups
entries.s: Before you start the new summer job at the café, you need to sign an official paper _____ with the company owner.

entries.d: To be in charge of or to guide
entries.s: The experienced tour guide will _____ us through the narrow streets of the old part of the historic city for two hours.

entries.d: A sweet white or brown substance used in food and drinks
entries.s: Do you take _____ in your morning tea or do you prefer it without anything sweet, just plain?

entries.d: To receive payment for work that you do
entries.s: She _____ some extra pocket money by walking her neighbour's big dog in the park every afternoon after school.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with EARN?

entries.d: To keep money so you can buy something later
entries.s: I'm _____ up hard for a new pair of expensive trainers that cost €120 — I put aside €10 every week from my pocket money.

entries.d: To write your name on a document
entries.s: Please _____ your full name at the bottom of the form with a black pen to confirm you agree with everything written.
